mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger corporate earnings downgrades, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.25 percentage points within one month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and consumer credit expansion indicates broad-based aggregate demand acceleration, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over a 3-year rolling window, or individual series show equal or stronger predictive power in isolation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase reflects sustained capacity utilization and pricing power, which supports corporate margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO growth exceeds the 75th percentile of its historical distribution, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in core consumer price inflation above a critical threshold erodes real purchasing power and forces aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The index threshold crossing consistently coincides with positive equity returns, or the forward return differential between high and low inflation regimes is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly correlates with expanding corporate revenues, creating a persistent positive drift in equity valuations that manifests as positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between quarterly GDP growth and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.10 over a 5-year window, or forward returns are flat during consecutive quarters of GDP contraction.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong nonfarm payroll growth and a stable unemployment rate indicates a resilient labor market that sustains consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance when added to a single-proxy model, or forward returns are negative when payroll growth exceeds 200k but unemployment rises simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: During an active monetary tightening cycle, elevated short-term interest rates increase the opportunity cost of holding equities, which suppresses risk appetite and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or statistically unchanged when the funds rate exceeds the 4.0 percent threshold, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during historical tightening episodes.
